entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: The legume species Cajanus cajan (L.) Millsp. used for hybrid development.

entities.name: CGMS
entities.description: Cytoplasmic Genetic Male Sterility system used to develop hybrids.

entities.name: SHORT DURATION
entities.description: The characteristic of the hybrids to mature in a shorter time.

entities.name: HETEROSIS
entities.description: The phenomenon of hybrid vigor where offspring exhibit superior traits over parents.

entities.name: HAND POLLINATION
entities.description: The technique used to cross CMS lines and testers.

entities.name: CMS LINES
entities.description: A-lines used as female parents in hybrid production.

entities.name: TESTERS
entities.description: R-lines used as male parents in hybrid production.

entities.name: VL ARHAR1
entities.description: A standard check variety of pigeonpea.

entities.name: ICPL 161
entities.description: A standard check variety of pigeonpea.

entities.name: RANDOMIZED BLOCK DESIGN
entities.description: The experimental design used for evaluating hybrids.

entities.name: KHARIF 2016-17
entities.description: The specific cropping season and year of the study.

entities.name: HETEROBELTIOSIS
entities.description: A type of heterosis where the hybrid outperforms the better parent.

entities.name: STANDARD HETEROSIS
entities.description: Heterosis measured relative to standard check varieties.

entities.name: ICPA 2039
entities.description: A pigeonpea line used in crosses.

entities.name: ICPL 90048
entities.description: A pigeonpea line used in crosses.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD PER PLANT
entities.description: A quantitative measure of productivity per individual plant.

entities.name: YIELD CONTRIBUTING CHARACTERS
entities.description: Morphological or physiological traits that influence overall grain yield.

entities.name: STABILITY
entities.description: The consistency of hybrid performance across different environments.
